description:
You are a system administrator who has decided to store the **backup-schedule** configuration for all servers under a single directory.  
    Your goal is to create (or overwrite, if it already exists) the following two files by issuing **one single terminal command**:

    • /home/user/service_configs/backup_schedule.yaml  
    • /home/user/service_configs/backup_schedule.toml  

    1. If the directory  /home/user/service_configs  does not already exist, your command must create it.  
    2. The YAML file must contain exactly the four-line block below—nothing more, nothing less; no leading or trailing blank lines and no trailing whitespace on any line (spaces after the last visible character are considered wrong):

        backup:
          enabled: true
          frequency: "daily"
          retention: 14
          destination: "/var/backups"

    3. The TOML file must contain exactly the five-line block below—again, no extra or missing blank lines, and no trailing whitespace on any line:

        [backup]
        enabled = true
        frequency = "daily"
        retention = 14
        destination = "/var/backups"

    4. Both files should end with a single UNIX newline (line-feed, \n).  
    5. File permissions must be 0644 (rw-r--r--).  
    6. You must accomplish everything with **one** shell command (it may be a compound command joined with &&, a here-document, etc., but it must be submitted to the shell in one shot).

    Automated tests will verify:

    • The presence of /home/user/service_configs/backup_schedule.yaml and /home/user/service_configs/backup_schedule.toml.  
    • That each file’s content matches **exactly** what is shown above, byte-for-byte.  
    • That each file ends with exactly one '\n'.  
    • That both files have permission mode 0644.

    Hint: a here-document or printf is usually the cleanest way to write multi-line content in a single command.
truth:
<!-- Secret data used by the grader -->
    <expected_files>
        <file path="/home/user/service_configs/backup_schedule.yaml" mode="0644"><![CDATA[
backup:
  enabled: true
  frequency: "daily"
  retention: 14
  destination: "/var/backups"
]]></file>
        <file path="/home/user/service_configs/backup_schedule.toml" mode="0644"><![CDATA[
[backup]
enabled = true
frequency = "daily"
retention = 14
destination = "/var/backups"
]]></file>
    </expected_files>
